PRE_PROCESSING.description: Validates the incoming roster file structure, format, and basic field completeness before any transformation begins.
PRE_PROCESSING.common_failures: Missing required fields, unsupported file format, malformed headers, encoding issues.

MAPPING_APPROVAL.description: Maps incoming provider fields from the source system schema to the internal canonical schema. Requires human or automated approval before proceeding.
MAPPING_APPROVAL.common_failures: Unmapped fields, schema mismatch between source system and internal standard, unapproved mapping templates.

ISF_GENERATION.description: Creates the Internal Standard Format record — the canonical provider record used by all downstream systems.
ISF_GENERATION.common_failures: Transformation errors, missing mandatory ISF fields, data type mismatches.

DART_GENERATION.description: Generates the DART transaction record for downstream SPS load. This is the most commonly stuck stage.
DART_GENERATION.common_failures: DART schema violations, NPI format errors, taxonomy code mismatches, payer rule violations.

DART_UI_VALIDATION.description: Human-in-the-loop review step where the generated DART transaction is validated before final SPS load. Can introduce delays if reviewers are backlogged.
DART_UI_VALIDATION.common_failures: Reviewer backlog, validation rule failures, manual rejection by reviewer.

SPS_LOAD.description: Final load of the provider record into the provider master system. Success here means the provider is live in the system.
SPS_LOAD.common_failures: Duplicate provider records, constraint violations in SPS, downstream system unavailability.

9.label: STOPPED
9.description: Pipeline has halted entirely. Manual intervention is required to resume. This is the most critical status.
9.action: Escalate immediately to pipeline ops team.

45.label: DART_REVIEW
45.description: File is awaiting human validation in the DART UI step. Pipeline is paused pending reviewer action.
45.action: Check reviewer queue. Escalate if waiting more than SLA threshold.

49.label: DART_GENERATION
49.description: File is actively generating DART transactions. If stuck here longer than AVG_DART_GENERATION_DURATION, flag as anomaly.
49.action: Compare DART_GEN_DURATION against AVG_DART_GENERATION_DURATION. If 2x or more, escalate.

99.label: RESOLVED
99.description: Pipeline completed successfully. All records processed and loaded into SPS.

FAIL_REC_CNT.meaning: Processing failure — the pipeline system itself could not handle this record.
FAIL_REC_CNT.implication: Indicates a pipeline or transformation problem, NOT a data quality issue. The system broke, not the source data.
FAIL_REC_CNT.action: Investigate pipeline logs, stage health flags, and system errors. Do not blame the submitting org.

REJ_REC_CNT.meaning: Compliance or validation rejection — the source data itself is non-conformant with required standards.
REJ_REC_CNT.implication: Indicates a data quality problem at the source. The submitting organization sent bad data.
REJ_REC_CNT.action: Contact the submitting org's data team. Identify which validation rules are failing. Check for recent regulatory changes.

SKIP_REC_CNT.meaning: Business rule skip — the record was intentionally excluded by deduplication or eligibility rules.
SKIP_REC_CNT.implication: Not necessarily a quality issue. Could be expected behavior for duplicate or ineligible providers.
SKIP_REC_CNT.action: Review skip reasons before escalating. Many skips are normal and expected.

SCS_REC_CNT.meaning: Successfully processed records — records that completed the full pipeline and loaded into SPS.
SCS_REC_CNT.implication: Higher is better. Low SCS_REC_CNT relative to TOT_REC_CNT indicates systemic issues.
SCS_REC_CNT.action: Monitor SCS_REC_CNT / TOT_REC_CNT ratio as primary quality metric.

TOT_REC_CNT.meaning: Total records in the roster file submitted for processing.
TOT_REC_CNT.implication: Baseline denominator for all rate calculations.
TOT_REC_CNT.action: Always use as denominator when computing rejection_rate and failure_rate.

record_type_semantics.critical_distinction: FAIL = pipeline problem (system fault). REJ = data quality problem (org fault). SKIP = business rule exclusion (often expected). Always distinguish these three in root cause analysis. Never conflate FAIL and REJ.

Green.meaning: Stage completed within normal duration bounds.
Green.threshold: Actual duration <= AVG duration for that stage.

Yellow.meaning: Stage duration is elevated above average. Monitor closely.
Yellow.threshold: Actual duration between 1x and 2x the AVG duration.
Yellow.action: Watch for progression to Red. No immediate escalation needed.

Red.meaning: Stage is critically delayed or failed. Immediate attention required.
Red.threshold: Actual duration > 2x the AVG duration, or stage has explicitly failed.
Red.action: Escalate to pipeline ops immediately. Check IS_STUCK and IS_FAILED flags.

health_flag_meanings.SPS_LOAD_HEALTH_note: SPS_LOAD_HEALTH is derived from SCS_PERCENT in the markets table, not just duration. A low success rate will flag SPS_LOAD_HEALTH as Red even if the stage completed quickly.

Medicare HMO.description: Federal managed care program for individuals aged 65 and older.
Medicare HMO.notes: CMS sets national standards. Changes are announced in advance.

Medicaid FFS.description: State-administered fee-for-service program for low-income individuals.
Medicaid FFS.notes: Highest compliance burden. Each state has its own rules. Most common source of rejection spikes due to state-level regulatory changes.

Commercial PPO.description: Private insurer preferred provider organization network.
Commercial PPO.notes: Lowest regulatory overhead. Rejections here are usually data quality issues, not regulatory.

Commercial EPO.description: Private insurer exclusive provider organization network.
Commercial EPO.notes: Similar to PPO in compliance burden. Network exclusivity rules apply.

Medicare Advantage.description: Privately administered Medicare plans approved by CMS.
Medicare Advantage.notes: Blend of CMS federal rules and private payer rules. Complexity is higher than standard Medicare HMO.

ros_table_columns.ID: Unique row identifier for this record in the database.
ros_table_columns.RO_ID: Roster Operation ID — the primary identifier for a pipeline run. Use this to track a specific file through all stages.
ros_table_columns.RA_FILE_DETAILS_ID: Foreign key linking to the raw file details table.
ros_table_columns.RA_ROSTER_DETAILS_ID: Foreign key linking to the roster details table.
ros_table_columns.RA_PLM_RO_PROF_DATA_ID: Foreign key linking to PLM profile data.
ros_table_columns.SRC_SYS: Source system that submitted this roster file. Different source systems have different schema conventions.
ros_table_columns.ORG_NM: Name of the provider organization that submitted this roster file. Primary entity for org-level analysis.
ros_table_columns.RUN_NO: Run number for this RO. RUN_NO=1 is the first attempt. RUN_NO>1 indicates retries. Use to measure retry effectiveness.
ros_table_columns.CNT_STATE: The US state this roster file is associated with. Primary join key to the markets table.
ros_table_columns.LOB: Line of business — Medicare HMO, Medicaid FFS, Commercial PPO, etc. Critical for compliance context.
ros_table_columns.FILE_RECEIVED_DT: Timestamp when the roster file was received by the system.
ros_table_columns.FILE_STATUS_CD: Current pipeline status code. See file_status_codes for mappings (9=STOPPED, 45=DART_REVIEW, 49=DART_GEN, 99=RESOLVED).
ros_table_columns.LATEST_STAGE_NM: The most recent pipeline stage this RO has reached or is currently in.
ros_table_columns.RA_PLM_RO_FILE_DATA_CREATION: Timestamp when the PLM file data record was created.
ros_table_columns.PRE_PROCESSING_END_DT: Timestamp when pre-processing stage ended. NULL if still in progress.
ros_table_columns.MAPPING_APPRVD_AT: Timestamp when mapping was approved. NULL if not yet approved.
ros_table_columns.ISF_GEN_END_DT: Timestamp when ISF generation ended. NULL if still in progress.
ros_table_columns.DART_GEN_END_DT: Timestamp when DART generation ended. NULL if still in progress.
ros_table_columns.RELEASED_TO_DART_UI_DT: Timestamp when file was released to DART UI for human validation.
ros_table_columns.DART_UI_VALIDATION_START_DT: Timestamp when DART UI validation started.
ros_table_columns.DART_UI_VALIDATION_END_DT: Timestamp when DART UI validation ended.
ros_table_columns.SPS_LOAD_END_DT: Timestamp when SPS load ended. NULL if still in progress.
ros_table_columns.PRE_PROCESSING_DURATION: Duration of pre-processing stage in minutes.
ros_table_columns.MAPPING_APROVAL_DURATION: Duration of mapping approval stage in minutes.
ros_table_columns.ISF_GEN_DURATION: Duration of ISF generation stage in minutes.
ros_table_columns.DART_GEN_DURATION: Duration of DART generation stage in minutes.
ros_table_columns.DART_REVIEW_DURATION: Duration of DART review stage in minutes.
ros_table_columns.DART_UI_VALIDATION_DURATION: Duration of DART UI validation stage in minutes.
ros_table_columns.SPS_LOAD_DURATION: Duration of SPS load stage in minutes.
ros_table_columns.AVG_DART_GENERATION_DURATION: Historical average DART generation duration for this org/LOB. Use as baseline for anomaly detection.
ros_table_columns.AVG_DART_UI_VLDTN_DURATION: Historical average DART UI validation duration. Use as baseline.
ros_table_columns.AVG_SPS_LOAD_DURATION: Historical average SPS load duration. Use as baseline.
ros_table_columns.AVG_ISF_GENERATION_DURATION: Historical average ISF generation duration. Use as baseline.
ros_table_columns.PRE_PROCESSING_HEALTH: Health flag for pre-processing stage: Green, Yellow, or Red.
ros_table_columns.MAPPING_APROVAL_HEALTH: Health flag for mapping approval stage: Green, Yellow, or Red.
ros_table_columns.ISF_GEN_HEALTH: Health flag for ISF generation stage: Green, Yellow, or Red.
ros_table_columns.DART_GEN_HEALTH: Health flag for DART generation stage: Green, Yellow, or Red.
ros_table_columns.DART_REVIEW_HEALTH: Health flag for DART review stage: Green, Yellow, or Red.
ros_table_columns.DART_UI_VALIDATION_HEALTH: Health flag for DART UI validation stage: Green, Yellow, or Red.
ros_table_columns.SPS_LOAD_HEALTH: Health flag for SPS load stage. Derived from SCS_PERCENT — low success rate = Red.
ros_table_columns.IS_STUCK: Boolean flag. 1 = this RO is currently stuck and not progressing. Primary flag for triage.
ros_table_columns.RA_BTCH_RUN_ID: Batch run identifier — groups ROs that were processed together in the same batch.
ros_table_columns.IS_FAILED: Boolean flag. 1 = this RO has explicitly failed and cannot proceed without intervention.
ros_table_columns.FAILURE_STATUS: Text description of the failure reason. Use for root cause classification.
ros_table_columns.LATEST_OBJECT_RUN_DT: Timestamp of the most recent activity on this RO.
ros_table_columns.CREAT_DT: Timestamp when this record was created in the database.
ros_table_columns.CREAT_USER_ID: User ID that created this record.
ros_table_columns.LAST_UPDT_DT: Timestamp of the last update to this record.
ros_table_columns.LAST_UPDT_USER_ID: User ID that last updated this record.

markets_table_columns.MONTH: The month this market metric snapshot covers. Format: YYYY-MM or month name.
markets_table_columns.MARKET: The market identifier — typically maps to CNT_STATE in the ros table. Primary join key.
markets_table_columns.FIRST_ITER_SCS_CNT: Number of records that succeeded on the first processing attempt, before any retries.
markets_table_columns.FIRST_ITER_FAIL_CNT: Number of records that failed on the first processing attempt.
markets_table_columns.NEXT_ITER_SCS_CNT: Number of records that succeeded on retry attempts after the first failure.
markets_table_columns.NEXT_ITER_FAIL_CNT: Number of records that failed even after retry attempts.
markets_table_columns.OVERALL_SCS_CNT: Total successful records across all iterations. Primary success metric.
markets_table_columns.OVERALL_FAIL_CNT: Total failed records across all iterations.
markets_table_columns.SCS_PERCENT: Overall success percentage for this market and month. Primary KPI. Below 85% is considered below threshold.
markets_table_columns.IS_ACTIVE: Boolean. 1 = this market is currently active and being monitored.
markets_table_columns.CREAT_DT: Timestamp when this record was created.
markets_table_columns.CREAT_USER_ID: User ID that created this record.
markets_table_columns.LAST_UPDT_USER_ID: User ID that last updated this record.

derived_columns.rejection_rate: Computed as REJ_REC_CNT / TOT_REC_CNT. Primary data quality metric per RO.
derived_columns.failure_rate: Computed as FAIL_REC_CNT / TOT_REC_CNT. Primary pipeline health metric per RO.
derived_columns.health_score: Weighted composite of health flags and rates. Lower = worse. Used for ranking ROs by severity.
derived_columns.is_anomaly: Boolean. Set to 1 if any stage duration exceeds 2x its historical average for that stage.
derived_columns.dominant_problem: Set to 'compliance' if REJ_REC_CNT > FAIL_REC_CNT, else 'processing'. Drives root cause direction.
derived_columns.below_threshold: Boolean on markets table. Set to 1 if SCS_PERCENT < 85.
derived_columns.retry_lift: Computed as NEXT_ITER_SCS_CNT / FIRST_ITER_SCS_CNT. Measures how much retries improved outcomes.
derived_columns.trend_direction: Direction of SCS_PERCENT change vs previous month. 'improving', 'declining', or 'stable'.

cross_layer_join.description: The ros and markets tables are joined on CNT_STATE = MARKET to connect file-level pipeline issues to market-level business outcomes.
cross_layer_join.use_case: When a market drops below threshold, join to ros table to identify which specific files and orgs are driving the degradation.
cross_layer_join.important_note: One market can have multiple active ROs. Always aggregate RO metrics when joining to market level.

anomaly_detection_rules.high_rejection: rejection_rate > 0.30 (30%) is a high rejection threshold. Above 0.50 is critical.
anomaly_detection_rules.stage_duration_anomaly: Any stage duration > 2x its AVG_*_DURATION column is flagged as anomaly.
anomaly_detection_rules.complete_validation_failure: FAILURE_STATUS = 'Complete Validation Failure' indicates total record non-conformance. Highest severity rejection type.

web_search_triggers.rejection_spike: When rejection_rate > 0.30 with no Red health flags and IS_STUCK=0, search for regulatory changes: 'CMS Medicaid provider data submission requirements [STATE] [YEAR]'
web_search_triggers.unknown_failure_status: When FAILURE_STATUS value is not in known list, search: 'provider roster [FAILURE_STATUS] compliance meaning'
web_search_triggers.unknown_org: When ORG_NM appears in anomaly list with no prior episodic context, search: '[ORG_NM] healthcare provider'
